chapter_title.ar: باب مَا يُعَايِنُ الْمُؤْمِنُ وَ الْكَافِرُ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – What the Believer and the Infidel see

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/296)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (13/296)
text: سَمِعْتُ أَبَا عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَقُولُ إِذَا بَلَغَتْ نَفْسُ أَحَدِكُمْ هَذِهِ وَ أَوْمَأَ بِيَدِهِ إِلَى حَلْقِهِ قَرَّتْ عَيْنُهُ .

en.hubeali: And from him, from Safwan, from Jaroud Bin Al Munzar who said,

‘I heard Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> saying: ‘When the soul of one of you reaches this (point)’, and he<sup>asws</sup> gestured with his<sup>asws</sup> hand to his<sup>asws</sup> throat, his eyes would be delighted’.
en.sarwar: 14. It is narrated from him (narrator of the above Hadith) from Safwan from Jarud ibn al-Mundhir who has said the following: “I heard abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, say, ‘When the soul of one of you reaches here’ - pointing to his throat - ‘his eyes become delightful.’”
